items.reason: 20 decisions (DEC-001 through DEC-020) each have id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and projects_to populated. The new platform-shape decisions (DEC-011 through DEC-020) cover dictionary generation direction, cross-spec field reconciliation, enum unification, privacy class closure, and migration generation as required.
decisions-pending.md contains PEND-001 (target 2026-05-15) and PEND-005 (target 2026-05-03), each with owner (Andy), blocker, and one-line rationale. No silent TBDs visible.

Each decision's consequences names concrete artifacts removed. E.g., DEC-001 'Removes per-function subject-to-person merge branches from supabase/functions/lti-launch-handler/index.ts, supabase/functions/caliper-event-ingestion/index.ts'; DEC-007 'Removes duplicate source-ID columns from every public table'; DEC-016 'Removes direct edits to dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json...'. Each also includes 'Why not collapse' justifications.

Decisions consistently bracket the design space with credible alternatives. DEC-001 considers separate tables vs collapsing all identities vs the chosen middle; DEC-005 considers AGS-only canonical model (a real architectural choice) vs source-native peer stores vs the chosen separation. The aggressive options are choices production platforms actually make.

data/data-dictionary.seed.json declares the upstream projection_policy citing DEC-016. scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py --check is invoked by VERIFY.md and check_dictionary_artifacts.py. projection_summary lists 5 specs, 59 objects, 574 fields generated from the seed; per-spec files are projections, not hand-edits.

canonical_objects.canonical.identity.person.email lists projections to dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.email, dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor.email, dictionary/qti-core.v1.json#qti_candidate.email, and dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership.email — the four required overlap targets resolve to one canonical field at the data level.

canonical.identity.person is a first-class canonical_object record in data/data-dictionary.seed.json with its own fields (id, display_name, email, source_subject_id, identity_kind), allowed values (known_person/external_agent), and projections to OneRoster person, Caliper actor, QTI candidate, LTI membership and launch.subject_id. Unification is data, not just prose in DEC-001.

shared_enums in each per-spec dictionary include canonical_values, crosswalk, and reverse_crosswalk. E.g., grading_progress in dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json maps OneRoster fullyGraded/notSubmitted/submitted/partiallyGraded plus LTI Initialized/Started/InProgress/Submitted/Completed/Pending/PendingManual/Failed/NotReady to canonical values, with bidirectional reverse_crosswalk. Each enum field references shared_enum_id per DEC-018.

DEC-019 declares the closed list (public, operational, directory, education_record, behavioral, sensitive, credential, privacy_governance, system). scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py defines INVALID_PRIVACY_CLASSES = {'depends_on_contents','depends_on_entity'} and validate_privacy_class rejects any field outside the closed list, so the placeholders cannot recur.

data/data-dictionary.seed.json#migration_generation declares table_order and relationship_keys per DEC-020. scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py validates the graph (foreign_key target objects/fields, cardinality, ownership, delete_behavior) and emits supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql. --check runs in VERIFY.md, and the committed SQL header reads 'Generated by scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py'.

Every field, object, and shared_enum carries decision_id. check_dictionary_artifacts.py loads the decision-trace block from docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md and fails if any field references an unknown decision_id or is missing from a decision's produces_fields list, preventing orphans and broken links.

scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py, generate_qti_core.py, generate_case_core.py, generate_caliper_core.py, and generate_integration_governance_core.py emit docs/generated/*-dictionary.md and site/docs/*-dictionary.html plus OpenAPI from the dictionary. scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py verifies object headings, field columns, decision IDs, sourceStandards, and enum values appear in SQL/OpenAPI/Markdown/HTML; VERIFY.md runs both regeneration and the artifact check.

docs/lead-spec-accounting.md provides a Decision Citation Map naming each Lead area's decision_id and one-line rationale, and the decision register itself contains plain-language 'Why not collapse' bullets. However, the substance check rejects 'a link to a 900-line raw Markdown file as the only explanation,' and there is no per-decision rendered HTML page on the portal with anchors per decision — the main 'why' surface is the 1500-line standards-overlap-decisions.md file.
items.traced_cause: scripts/build_site_docs.py and site/docs/: render docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md as one HTML page per decision with stable anchors, and add short 'why this exists' prose pages for each load-bearing concept (canonical identity, enrollment vs membership, CASE-as-canonical) that link to the decision anchor.

items.reason: No docs/build-an-edtech-app.md (or equivalent end-to-end teaching-app guide) appears in README.md's Current Artifacts list, in the evidence file inventory, or in any portal index. Existing docs (supabase-hosted-database.md, lead-spec-accounting.md, dictionary-coverage-matrix.md) describe individual surfaces but never walk through the five required steps with copy-pasteable executables linked to dictionary entries.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: create the file with five executable sections (create_school, create_class, post_grade, link_case_standard, read_caliper_feed). Each step must include a curl/SQL example against the live Supabase REST or Edge Function URLs, and link every field name to its dictionary anchor and every enum value to its global shared_enum entry. The guide must also be linked from site/index.html and README.md.

items.reason: site/docs/index.html and per-spec generated HTML pages exist and the README/portal lists them, but no link-checker output, sitemap, or three-clicks-from-portal navigation evidence is present in the repo. There is no proof every dictionary object, every decision, and every API endpoint is reachable, and the buildability guide (a required navigation target) does not exist.
items.traced_cause: scripts/check_site_links.py (new): add a link checker that walks site/ HTML and asserts zero 404s and that every dictionary object/field anchor, every DEC-### anchor, every OpenAPI page, and the buildability guide is reachable from site/index.html within three clicks; wire it into VERIFY.md.

items.reason: Reading only docs/, a layperson cannot complete the five-step scenario. docs/supabase-hosted-database.md only documents read-only REST plus five Edge Functions (gradebook-bulk-submit, audited-roster-read, lti-launch-handler, caliper-event-ingestion, oauth-token-exchange); there is no documented endpoint to create a school, create a class, link to CASE, or read a Caliper activity feed. No build-an-edtech-app.md guide exists, so field shapes and enum values must be reverse-engineered from JSON dictionaries the rubric forbids the layperson from reading directly.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md plus the Edge Functions and CASE/Caliper read paths it depends on: write the guide with executable steps, and add (or document) the missing endpoints — POST organizations + classes (Edge Function or documented Supabase REST insert with RLS-permitted policy), CASE alignment lookup endpoint, and a Caliper-event-read endpoint. Each step must link to dictionary anchors per docs_include_buildability_guide.

items.reason: scripts/evaluate_platform.py defines DEFAULT_TRACED_CAUSES per rubric id, ensure_traced_causes() guarantees a non-empty traced_cause on every non-pass item, and normalize_buildability_gaps() builds a buildability_gaps array with traced_cause per gap. apply_prereq_blocks() converts unmet prereqs into 'blocked' rather than 'fail' with explicit prereq names.

VERIFY.md ends with 'python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json'. The evaluator writes counts, done, items with traced_cause, and buildability_gaps to that path, and codex_loop.py invokes run_evaluator before and after each iteration, logging counts.

scripts/codex_loop.py defines read_overrides() reading docs/loop-overrides.md, overrides_paused() halting before the next iteration when a bare PAUSE token appears outside HTML comments, and build_prompt() injects the cleaned content under a 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE...)' section in every prompt.

codex_loop.py terminates on 'after_eval.get("done")' both before any work ('Evaluator reports `done` before any work') and after each iteration ('Evaluator reports `done` after this iteration. Stopping the loop.'). It also stops on stall detection and max_iterations as safety rails. No numeric-threshold gating remains.

buildability_gaps.gap: No documented endpoint or curl/SQL example to create an organization. Supabase REST is read-only by RLS, no create-organization Edge Function exists, and no doc walks through the request shape.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md and a new supabase/functions/create-organization/index.ts (or documented authenticated Supabase REST insert path with an INSERT RLS policy on public.organizations): add the endpoint and document the JSON body using dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#organization fields with anchor links.

buildability_gaps.gap: Same as create_school — no documented POST for classes/courses/enrollments. The layperson cannot enroll the teacher and ten students.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md and supabase/functions/create-class/index.ts (or equivalent): document an authenticated POST that creates course, class, and enrollment rows, with each field linked to dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#class, #course, #enrollment.

buildability_gaps.gap: gradebook-bulk-submit Edge Function exists with a curl example in docs/supabase-hosted-database.md, but the example is not framed as part of an end-to-end teaching app and field names are not linked to dictionary anchors or shared_enum values (e.g., scoreStatus 'fullyGraded').
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: copy the gradebook-bulk-submit curl example into the guide and link scoreStatus to the grading_progress shared_enum and lineItemId/personId to dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#line_item.id and #person.id.

buildability_gaps.gap: DEC-012 explicitly defers CASE runtime; no endpoint or doc shows how to attach a CASE URI to an assignment. The dictionary defines case_item.identifier and qti_alignment.target_identifier but no API exposes alignment writes.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: Either ship a CASE alignment Edge Function (e.g., supabase/functions/attach-case-alignment) writing to a dictionary-backed alignments table generated from data/data-dictionary.seed.json, or sharpen DEC-012 to commit a target_date for this slice and add a 'Pending until PEND-001' note in the guide so the gap is honest rather than silent.

buildability_gaps.gap: supabase/functions/caliper-event-ingestion only ingests events; no read endpoint or feed query is documented. The dictionary describes caliper_event but the layperson cannot retrieve a class's activity stream.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: supabase/functions/caliper-event-feed/index.ts (new) or documented authenticated Supabase REST view over caliper_event with class-scoped RLS, then docs/build-an-edtech-app.md links every field to dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_event anchors.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. QTI and CASE are doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated Edge Function receipt/governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, Caliper raw-event projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.
